evidence: Right-hand driving side on a multi-lane highway is consistent with Continental Europe.
The presence of a large 3-blade wind turbine in a flat agricultural setting is highly characteristic of the Danish and Northern German landscapes.
Solid white outer edge lines and broken white inner lane lines are standard highway markings for European expressways.
Flat agricultural fields with deciduous trees and bushes suggest a Northern European temperate climate.
Double-rail metal guardrails are a standard safety feature on Danish and German highway networks.
A white car hood without a visible antenna is common in recent Google Street View coverage (Gen 4) in Denmark.
contradictions: The lack of specific signage or language on the road makes it difficult to definitively distinguish between Denmark, Northern Germany, and Poland.
